sha_convention: bytes on disk as-is (binary read, no line-ending normalization)
pass_type: adversarial: attestation loopholes A1-A3, injector-identity edge cases, gain-leg goalpost risks

findings.finding: 'founders off' has two sub-checks: (a) process snapshot (zero claude.exe/codex sessions interacting) and (b) sqlite outbound count == 0. These are independent: a founder process could be alive without sending mail (a passes, b fails) or a scheduled mail could fire after the snapshot (a passes, b fails). The two checks are not coupled in time.
findings.fix: Sample both checks at the same timestamp; add a continuous outbound monitor for the full 120-min window, not just a start/end count.

findings.finding: Config-level check (zero cloud endpoints in config) does not equal runtime check (zero cloud calls made). A resident loop could dynamically load a cloud endpoint at runtime not listed in the static config. Network audit line in receipt must be a captured runtime network trace, not a config inspection.
findings.fix: Require a runtime network capture (e.g. strace/netstat log) proving zero external connections during the window, not just config inspection.

findings.finding: 'git status pre/post: no commits or working-tree mutations authored outside ember's own resident process' — git status does not show commit authorship. Outside commits already pushed would not appear in git status. To verify no outside-authored commits, git log --since=<start> with author filter is required.
findings.fix: Replace git status with: git log --since=<window_start> --until=<window_end> --format='%H %an' to verify all commits in window authored by ember's process identity.

findings.finding: Injector identity is not named in the spec. A1 excludes 'injector identity' from founder outbound count, but if the injector sends mails using a founder identity (e.g. as leo), ember's MAIL_REPLY responses to the injector would be counted as outbound from ember to leo — not excluded. Misattribution would fail A1 incorrectly.
findings.fix: Name the injector identity explicitly in the spec (e.g. 'injector' or a dedicated mailbox sender id). Require the replay rig to use a non-founder sender id.

findings.finding: Ember's MAIL_REPLY responses to the injector are outbound mails from ember. The A1 exclusion covers injector-sent mails (injector as sender), not mails sent TO the injector (injector as recipient). If the spec intends ember's event-leg replies to NOT count against A1's founder-outbound check, this needs explicit carve-out.
findings.fix: Clarify: 'outbound count == 0 excludes mails whose recipient is the injector identity (event-leg replies are expected)'.

findings.finding: The held-out bucket for gain measurement must be frozen BEFORE the micro-round starts. The spec says 'paired pre/post on the round's held-out bucket' but does not specify when the bucket is frozen. If selected after the round, cherry-picking a favorable bucket is possible.
findings.fix: Require: held-out bucket sha256 recorded in the receipt BEFORE round start (pre-round attestation). Mismatch between pre-bound sha and post-round evaluation sha = FAIL.

findings.finding: Ground truth source for the micro-round's verify step is not specified. If fetched from a cloud endpoint, it violates A2 (cloud off in loop path). If locally cached, the cache provenance must be frozen pre-window.
findings.fix: Specify ground truth source: either (a) a locally pinned file with sha256 in the receipt, or (b) the existing ledger/view mechanism (already local). Prohibit runtime cloud fetch.

findings.finding: Micro-round duration is not bounded. If the round takes >120 min it cannot complete inside the window. The spec allows the gain leg to run 'inside the window' with a 120-min window but no cap on round duration.
findings.fix: Add: micro-round must complete within <T> min of window start (e.g. leave 20 min for gain measurement). Or: state that if micro-round does not complete inside window, C52 = FAIL.

findings.finding: Timestamps in the receipt are self-attested by the orchestrator that assembles the receipt. No independent timestamp source required (e.g. signed clock, git commit timestamp). An orchestrator controlling receipt assembly could falsify window boundaries.
findings.fix: Anchor window start/end to verifiable external markers: git commit sha at start (pre-state) + end (post-state), both sha256-bound in the receipt.
